articles.title: My Mother
articles.author: Mary Hess Hackney
articles.text:
The trenchant beauty of her mother soul
Is ageless, and when one day she must leave,
And crucibles have taken of their toll,
I know that she will bid me not to grieve,
For while she waits across the narrow sea
Her guiding love will still envelop me.

articles.title: About Flowers
articles.author: Elaine Swain
articles.text:
Some covet baskets of roses,
Gardenias to twine in their hair;
They long to pin on an orchid
When they hear his step on the stair.

But give me short-stemmed poppies,
Plucked on a summer's day—
Held tightly in a tiny fist
Presented as "Mommie's bouquet."
